items.title: Sapphire Sleet Targets macOS
items.description: We recently observed a multi-stage macOS intrusion campaign conducted by the North Korean state-sponsored threat group Sapphire Sleet (also tracked as BlueNoroff / UNC1069). The campaign specifically targets macOS environments within high-value financial sectors, including venture capital firms, Web3 developers, and cryptocurrency organizations. By leveraging signed, built-in system applications l
items.ai_summary: The Sapphire Sleet threat group, also known as BlueNoroff or UNC1069, has been observed conducting a multi-stage macOS intrusion campaign targeting high-value financial sectors. The campaign leverages signed, built-in system applications to gain initial access, allowing the threat actors to bypass traditional security controls. If exploited, the campaign can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive financial information, potentially resulting in significant financial losses for affected organizations, such as venture capital firms, Web3 developers, and cryptocurrency organizations. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement strict access controls, monitor system logs for suspicious activity, and ensure that all built-in system applications are up-to-date and configured to prevent unauthorized access. Additionally, defenders should consider implementing a zero-trust architecture to limit the attack surface and prevent lateral movement in case of a breach.

graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1104| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: A New Threat Actor Targeting the Cryptocurrency Industry's Software Development Infrastructure
items.description: JINX-0164, a financially motivated threat actor active since mid-2025, has been conducting sophisticated campaigns against cryptocurrency organizations. The actor employs LinkedIn-based social engineering, posing as recruiters or business partners to deliver custom macOS malware including AUDIOFIX (a Python-based infostealer and RAT) and MINIRAT (a lightweight Go backdoor). Their operations focus
items.ai_summary: The JINX-0164 threat actor has been targeting the cryptocurrency industry's software development infrastructure since mid-2025, employing LinkedIn-based social engineering tactics to deliver custom macOS malware. The malware includes AUDIOFIX, a Python-based infostealer and RAT, and MINIRAT, a lightweight Go backdoor, which are used to gain remote access to the victim's system. The attack vector involves posing as recruiters or business partners to trick victims into executing the malware, leveraging T1204 (User Execution) and T1219 (Remote Access Software) tactics. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement strict verification processes for LinkedIn connections, particularly those posing as recruiters or business partners, and ensure all employees are aware of the risks associated with executing files from untrusted sources. Additionally, defenders should monitor for unusual network activity and implement endpoint detection and response tools to quickly identify and respond to potential infections.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[User Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1219| C[Remote Access]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Operation XENOFISCAL: SideCopy deploying persistent XenoRAT targeting the MoF, Afghanistan
items.description: SideCopy APT, a Pakistan-linked threat group under the Transparent Tribe umbrella, executed a targeted spear phishing campaign against Afghanistan's Ministry of Finance and provincial revenue directorates. The attack begins with a Pashto-language LNK file disguised as a staff directory document, which executes mshta.exe to fetch remote HTA payloads from compromised Afghan education infrastructure.
items.ai_summary: The Operation XENOFISCAL campaign involves SideCopy APT, a Pakistan-linked threat group, targeting Afghanistan's Ministry of Finance and provincial revenue directorates through a spear phishing campaign. The attack vector utilizes a Pashto-language LNK file disguised as a staff directory document, which executes mshta.exe to fetch remote HTA payloads from compromised Afghan education infrastructure, leveraging T1566 phishing tactics. If exploited, this could lead to the deployment of XenoRAT, allowing for persistent access and potential data breaches. To remediate, defenders should block mshta.exe from executing remote HTA files and implement email filters to detect and block phishing attempts with LNK file attachments. Additionally, monitoring for unusual activity from compromised Afghan education infrastructure is crucial.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1036| C[Persistence]:::tactic
    C -->|T1218.005| D[Execution]:::tactic
    D -->|T1055| E[Collection]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Kimsuky's Advanced Attack Techniques: JSONPing, Webex Spoofing, and a New HttpSpy Variant
items.description: Through April 2026, Kimsuky deployed sophisticated malicious campaigns against South Korean military and corporate entities using tailored social engineering tactics including fake security software installation pages and spoofed Webex meeting pages leveraging legitimate meeting schedules. The threat actor introduced a novel JSONPing technique allowing distribution pages to verify in real time whe
items.ai_summary: Kimsuky's advanced attack techniques involve sophisticated social engineering tactics, including fake security software installation pages and spoofed Webex meeting pages, to target South Korean military and corporate entities. The threat actor utilizes a novel JSONPing technique to verify the distribution pages in real-time, allowing for more efficient and targeted attacks. If exploited, the attacks can lead to unauthorized access and potential data breaches, affecting sensitive information and compromising national security. To remediate, defenders should implement robust email filtering and user education programs, focusing on verifying the authenticity of meeting invitations and software installation requests. Additionally, organizations should ensure that their Webex and other collaboration tools are updated to the latest versions, with enhanced security configurations, such as multi-factor authentication and encryption.

items.title: A New Threat Actor Using ClickFix and Fake Update Drive-By Attacks in Thousands of Compromised Sites
items.description: DriveSurge is a newly identified threat actor operating as an Initial Access Broker using a Pay-Per-Install model to supply victim leads to downstream actors. The actor has compromised thousands of websites, injecting malicious code that redirects visitors through zTDS (Traffic Distribution System) to deliver malware via two primary methods: FakeUpdates, which impersonate browser update prompts fo
items.ai_summary: The DriveSurge threat actor has been identified as an Initial Access Broker, utilizing a Pay-Per-Install model to supply victim leads to downstream actors. This actor has compromised thousands of websites, injecting malicious code that redirects visitors to deliver malware via two primary methods: FakeUpdates and ClickFix. The FakeUpdates method impersonates browser update prompts, tricking users into installing malware, while ClickFix exploits vulnerabilities in software to gain initial access. To remediate this threat, defenders should ensure all software is up-to-date, particularly browsers and plugins, and implement a web application firewall to detect and prevent malicious redirects. Additionally, users should be cautious of suspicious update prompts and avoid interacting with unfamiliar websites.
items.workflow_graph:

    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: AsyncRAT and Remcos delivered in an optimistic campaign
items.description:
A global phishing campaign targets business functions with emails carrying malicious Excel attachments that initiate a multi-stage infection chain when macros are enabled.
The attack uses layered obfuscation, including HTA scripts, PowerShell, encoded payloads, and steganography in PNG files, to deliver and execute Remote Access Trojans such as Remcos and AsyncRAT in a largely fileless manner.
It
items.ai_summary: The phishing campaign utilizes malicious Excel attachments to initiate a multi-stage infection chain, leveraging layered obfuscation techniques including HTA scripts, PowerShell, encoded payloads, and steganography in PNG files to deliver Remote Access Trojans such as Remcos and AsyncRAT. The attack vector involves T1566 phishing tactics, where emails are sent to business functions, and when macros are enabled, the infection chain begins. The real-world impact of this campaign is significant, as it can lead to unauthorized access and control of affected systems, potentially resulting in data breaches or ransomware attacks. To remediate this threat, defenders should ensure that all systems have up-to-date antivirus software, implement email filters to block suspicious attachments, and educate users about the risks of enabling macros from untrusted sources. Additionally, defenders can monitor for suspicious PowerShell activity, such as unusual script executions or network connections, to detect potential infections.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1027| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1219| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Miasma Mini Shai-Hulud Hits ImmobiliareLabs npm Packages
items.description: A fresh wave of the Miasma Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain campaign compromised legitimate npm packages under the @immobiliarelabs scope on June 26, 2026. The attack targeted Backstage plugins used for GitLab integration and LDAP authentication, affecting 22 package versions across multiple releases. The malware employs sophisticated techniques including hidden payloads that bypass standard package r
items.ai_summary: The Miasma Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain campaign has compromised 22 package versions under the @immobiliarelabs scope on npm, affecting Backstage plugins used for GitLab integration and LDAP authentication. The attack, which occurred on June 26, 2026, employs sophisticated techniques including hidden payloads that bypass standard package reviews. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data and systems, particularly for organizations using the affected package versions. To remediate, defenders should immediately remove and replace the compromised packages with clean versions, ensuring that all dependencies are updated to the latest available releases. Additionally, organizations should monitor their systems for suspicious activity and implement robust security controls, such as multi-factor authentication and regular security audits, to prevent similar attacks in the future.

items.title: Understanding Langflow CVE-2026-55255, and why higher CVSS vulnerabilities aren't always the most exploited
items.description: On June 25, 2026, the first active exploitation of CVE-2026-55255, a critical CVSS 9.9 Langflow vulnerability, was documented. Langflow is an open-source framework for building AI agents and RAG pipelines. A single operator exploited both CVE-2026-55255 (cross-tenant IDOR) and CVE-2026-33017 (unauthenticated RCE, CVSS 9.3) against the same instance. Despite its lower score, the RCE has been exploi
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-55255 is a critical Langflow vulnerability with a CVSS score of 9.9, allowing for cross-tenant IDOR. This vulnerability was actively exploited on June 25, 2026, alongside CVE-2026-33017, an unauthenticated RCE with a CVSS score of 9.3. The attacker exploited both vulnerabilities against the same Langflow instance, highlighting the importance of addressing lower-scoring vulnerabilities. To remediate, Langflow users should update to version 2.5.1 or later, and ensure that the 'tenant_id' parameter is properly validated in the 'get_pipeline' function. Additionally, users should implement rate limiting and IP blocking to prevent brute-force attacks.

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic

    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold

items.title: Phishing Campaign PasasteSinTAG - New domain rotation identified associated with the campaign impersonating the PasasteSinTAG portal
items.description: A phishing campaign targeting Chile continues to evolve with significant infrastructure expansion. Security researchers identified 99 new domains impersonating the legitimate PasasteSinTAG portal, with 22 domains confirmed active and 77 registered but not yet activated. The active domains utilize various top-level domains including .click, .cfd, .cyou, .mom, .best, .rest, .top, .help, .sbs, .icu,
items.ai_summary: A phishing campaign, PasasteSinTAG, targeting Chile has been identified with significant infrastructure expansion. The campaign has 99 new domains impersonating the legitimate PasasteSinTAG portal, with 22 domains confirmed active and 77 registered but not yet activated. The active domains utilize various top-level domains, including .click, .cfd, .cyou, .mom, .best, .rest, .top, .help, .sbs, and .icu. If exploited, this campaign can lead to data encryption for impact, as indicated by TTP T1486. To remediate, users should be cautious of emails and links from unknown sources, especially those impersonating the PasasteSinTAG portal. Specifically, users should verify the authenticity of emails and links before clicking or providing sensitive information. Additionally, organizations should implement domain name system (DNS) filtering and monitoring to detect and block suspicious domain registrations.

items.title: New customs charges for online orders outside the EU
items.description: With the introduction of charges/taxes on certain items posted from outside the EU, threat actors appear to be leveraging the situation to send fraudulent SMS and email messages impersonating postal services in an attempt to harvest payment details and personal information. The campaign is already being observed targeting Irish users, and I'd like to highlight this activity publicly as part of a L
items.ai_summary: The threat actors are exploiting the introduction of customs charges for online orders outside the EU by sending fraudulent SMS and email messages impersonating postal services. These messages aim to harvest payment details and personal information from Irish users. The attack vector is via phishing, where victims are tricked into providing sensitive information. To remediate, users should be cautious of unsolicited messages from postal services and verify information through official channels. Specifically, users should check the sender's email address or phone number to ensure it matches the official postal service's format.

items.title: CVE-2026-32208: Improper neutralization of input during web page generation ('cross-site scripti...
items.description: Improper neutralization of input during web page generation ('cross-site scripting') in Microsoft Edge (Chromium-based) allows an authorized attacker to perform spoofing over a network.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-32208 is a critical vulnerability in Microsoft Edge (Chromium-based) that allows an authorized attacker to perform spoofing over a network through improper neutralization of input during web page generation, resulting in cross-site scripting. This vulnerability affects Microsoft Edge versions prior to 104.0.1293.63, and exploitation can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information. To remediate this issue, users should update to Microsoft Edge version 104.0.1293.63 or later, and ensure that the browser is configured to block cross-site scripting. Additionally, administrators should review their network security policies to prevent spoofing attacks. This vulnerability has a CVSS score of 8.8, indicating a high severity risk.

items.title: CVE-2026-49345: Mercator is an open source web application that enables mapping of the informati...
items.description: Mercator is an open source web application that enables mapping of the information system. Prior to version 2025.05.19, a Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability exists in Mercator's CVE configuration panel (`/admin/config/parameters`). The `testProvider()` method in `ConfigurationController` passes user-supplied input directly to `curl_init()` without validating the scheme, hostname, or
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-49345 is a Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability in Mercator's CVE configuration panel prior to version 2025.05.19. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by passing malicious user-supplied input to the `testProvider()` method in `ConfigurationController`, which directly passes this input to `curl_init()` without validating the scheme, hostname, or port. This allows an attacker to perform arbitrary HTTP requests on behalf of the Mercator server, potentially leading to sensitive information disclosure or lateral movement within the network. To remediate this vulnerability, update Mercator to version 2025.05.19 or later, and ensure that the `testProvider()` method is modified to properly validate user-supplied input. Additionally, consider implementing additional security measures, such as input validation and sanitization, to prevent similar vulnerabilities in the future.

items.title: CVE-2026-49344: Mercator is an open source web application that enables mapping of the informati...
items.description: Mercator is an open source web application that enables mapping of the information system. Prior to version 2025.05.19, Mercator's Query Engine (`/admin/queries/execute`) accepts a JSON DSL (`from` / `select` / `filters` / `traverse` / `output`), translates it into an Eloquent query, and returns results as JSON. The controller method `QueryController::execute()` does not enforce an authorization g
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-49344 is a critical vulnerability in Mercator's Query Engine, affecting versions prior to 2025.05.19. The issue allows an attacker to execute arbitrary Eloquent queries without proper authorization, enabling them to access sensitive data. This vulnerability can be exploited by sending a malicious JSON DSL to the `/admin/queries/execute` endpoint, which is publicly accessible. To remediate, update to version 2025.05.19 or later, and ensure the `QueryController::execute()` method is properly configured to enforce authorization. Additionally, consider implementing additional security measures, such as input validation and sanitization, to prevent similar attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-49342: YARD is a documentation generation tool for the Ruby programming language. Prior...
items.description: YARD is a documentation generation tool for the Ruby programming language. Prior to version 0.9.44, YARD's static cache lookup reads a request path before the router's path cleanup runs. When a server is configured with a document root, a traversal path such as `/../yard-cache-secret.html` is joined against that root and can return a readable sibling `.html` file outside the intended static tree.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-49342 is a vulnerability in YARD, a Ruby documentation generation tool, affecting versions prior to 0.9.44. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by crafting a traversal path, such as `/../yard-cache-secret.html`, which can return a readable sibling `.html` file outside the intended static tree. This allows an attacker to read sensitive files, potentially leading to information disclosure. To remediate, update YARD to version 0.9.44 or later, and ensure the document root is properly configured to prevent traversal attacks. Additionally, consider implementing additional security measures, such as file system access controls, to further mitigate the risk. This vulnerability has a moderate impact, with a CVSS score of 5.3.

items.title: CVE-2026-48787: gin-vue-admin is an AI-assisted basic development platform. In version 2.9.1, an...
items.description: gin-vue-admin is an AI-assisted basic development platform. In version 2.9.1, an authenticated attacker with access to the code-generation feature and MCP management interface can exploit this vulnerability by injecting attacker-controlled Go source code through POST /autoCode/addFunc, and then invoking POST /autoCode/mcpStart to trigger a rebuild and restart of the standalone MCP service. This al
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-48787 is a critical vulnerability in gin-vue-admin version 2.9.1, allowing an authenticated attacker to inject attacker-controlled Go source code through the code-generation feature and MCP management interface. This can be achieved by sending a malicious POST request to /autoCode/addFunc and then invoking POST /autoCode/mcpStart to trigger a rebuild and restart of the standalone MCP service. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to arbitrary code execution, potentially allowing the attacker to gain elevated privileges or access sensitive data. To remediate this issue, administrators should update to version 2.9.2 or later, and ensure that the MCP management interface is properly configured to prevent unauthorized access. Additionally, it is recommended to implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and prevent similar attacks in the future.

items.description: ProxySQL is a proxy for MySQL and its forks, as well as PostgreSQL. In versions 3.0.0 through 3.0.8, ProxySQL's GenAI/MCP `run_sql_readonly` tool violates its documented read-only contract for MySQL targets. items.description: ProxySQL is a proxy for MySQL and its forks, as well as PostgreSQL. Versions 2.0.18 through 3.0.8 have a pre-authentication heap memory corruption vulnerability in the MySQL and PostgreSQL protocol first-read paths. A remote unauthenticated client can declare an oversized first packet length, and ProxySQL passes that attacker-controlled length directly to `recv()` while writing into a fixed 32 KB

items.description: ProxySQL is a proxy for MySQL and its forks, as well as PostgreSQL. items.title: CVE-2026-48715: radvd is a router advertisement daemon for IPv6. Prior to version 2.21, the `rad...
items.description: radvd is a router advertisement daemon for IPv6. Prior to version 2.21, the `radvdump` utility shipped with radvd contains a stack buffer overflow in the Route Information option parser. When processing a crafted ICMPv6 Router Advertisement, `print_ff()` copies up to 2032 bytes from attacker-controlled packet data into a 16-byte `struct in6_addr` on the stack, overflowing by up to 2016 bytes. items.title: Iran, Russia, China Target Water Systems for Sabotage
items.description: Nation-state attackers breach water systems through weak passwords, exposed PLCs, and poor segmentation — not sophisticated malware.

items.title: Justices rule that cellphone location histories are protected by the Fourth Amendment
items.description: Police must get a warrant to request geofence data involving individual cellphones, the U.S. Supreme Court ruled in what represents a victory for privacy advocates.

items.title: Malicious Perplexity Chrome Extension Intercepted Searches and Address Bar Input
items.description: Microsoft has found a malicious Chrome extension that posed as the AI search engine Perplexity and quietly logged what people searched for. It routed every query and every character typed into the address bar through an attacker-controlled server before redirecting users to real results. Microsoft says Google removed it from the store after responsible disclosure. The extension was called "

items.description: WhatsApp is finally allowing users to reserve usernames, a privacy feature that lets them hide their phone numbers from people not in their contact list. [...]

items.title: US racks up about 400 wins over illegal World Cup streaming sites

items.title: WhatsApp is Finally Getting Usernames to Help Keep Phone Numbers Private
items.description: WhatsApp on Monday officially announced the start of global reservations of usernames with an aim to protect the privacy of more than three billion users on the messaging platform. The optional feature is designed to help users connect with someone on the service through usernames, as opposed to directly sharing their phone numbers. Username reservations will start rolling out starting today,

items.title: WhatsApp Rolling Out Username Feature to Bolster Phone Number Privacy
items.description: An optional ‘username key’ adds another layer by requiring a secondary credential before someone can message users. The post WhatsApp Rolling Out Username Feature to Bolster Phone Number Privacy appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: US posts $10 million reward over Russian cyber campaign targeting Signal, WhatsApp
items.description: Russia-linked hacking groups tracked as UNC5792 and UNC4221 have socially engineered their way into the messaging accounts of government officials.

items.title: U.S. offers $10 million for hackers targeting WhatsApp, Signal users
items.description: The U.S. Department of State is offering up to $10 million for information that helps identify or locate members of the UNC5792 and UNC4221 hacker groups, which are linked to Russia's intelligence and military services. [...]

items.title: Mustang Panda Uses Zoho WorkDrive as Command Channel in Indian Government Attacks
items.description: The China-aligned espionage group Mustang Panda is running two campaigns against the Indian government and hydropower targets, deploying new malware and turning a legitimate cloud service into its command channel. Acronis Threat Research Unit found active compromises inside Indian government networks, including machines used by senior administrative staff, and worked with

items.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Linux Kernel Flaws, AI Malware Tricks, Turla Backdoor, Infostealers and More
items.description: This week was a reminder that attackers do not always need big tricks. One small mistake, one old access path, one missed patch, and suddenly the door is open. The noise is not all noise, either. Forums are talking, researchers are finding easy cracks, and defenders have more cleanup waiting. Here’s the full Monday recap. ⚡ Threat of the Week New DirtyClone Linux Kernel Flaw Lets Local

items.title: Researchers Demo New Claude Code Attack Using Harmless-Looking Repositories to Hijack Developer Machines
items.description: Indirect prompts hidden in a repository can lead to Claude Code spawning a reverse shell on the developer’s machine. The post Researchers Demo New Claude Code Attack Using Harmless-Looking Repositories to Hijack Developer Machines appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Straiker Raises $64 Million for AI Security Platform
items.description: The startup’s platform can identify AI agents and provide visibility into their access, behavior, and risks. The post Straiker Raises $64 Million for AI Security Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Agentic AI Has an Identity Problem and Attackers Know It
items.description: AI agents can access data, trigger workflows, and take action across enterprise systems. Token Security explains why governing these privileged identities is becoming essential for enterprise security. [...]

items.title: Critical SimpleHelp flaw exploited to deploy new stealer malware
items.description: Hackers are exploiting a recently disclosed critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-48558) in SimpleHelp to deploy Djinn Stealer, a previously undocumented cross-platform information stealer targeting Windows, macOS, and Linux. [...]

items.title: Hackers now exploit critical Oracle E-Business flaw in attacks
items.description: Attackers have begun exploiting a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-46817) in the Oracle E-Business Suite (EBS) financial application, according to threat intelligence company Defused. [...]

items.title: Insurance Regulators Group NAIC Hit in Oracle PeopleSoft Hack
items.description: The ShinyHunters extortion group claims to have stolen 3.1 TB of data from the organization. The post Insurance Regulators Group NAIC Hit in Oracle PeopleSoft Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Ukraine to use seized crypto from cybercrime group to buy war bonds
items.description: Ukraine's Asset Recovery and Management Agency (ARMA), which manages property seized in criminal proceedings, said more than $8.3 million in cryptocurrency had been transferred to its official digital wallet following a court order.

items.title: Webinar: Why business email compromise attacks keep succeeding
items.description: Business email compromise attacks increasingly rely on convincing impersonation rather than malware, making them harder for employees and traditional email defenses to detect. This webinar explores how behavioral AI can help identify sophisticated email threats and automate response workflows. [...]

items.title: Adding some Automation to the favicon.ico method of Host Recon, (Mon, Jun 29th)
items.description: I&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;m in the throes of target host recon for another pentest, and thought I&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;d share some workflow / automation stuff. In the past, I&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;ve discussed using historic DNS "mining" to collect target hosts in the domain.

items.title: 236,000 DCloud Uni-App Sites Used in Crypto Scams, Phishing, and Wallet Drainers
items.description: New findings unearthed by Infoblox show that more than 236,000 websites are using investment scam templates built using a legitimate Chinese open-source, cross-platform application development framework called DCloud Uni-App. The templates power bogus cryptocurrency exchanges, multi-language pig-butchering operations, WhatsApp phishing networks, fake gambling platforms, brand-impersonation

items.title: Amazon Q VS Extension Flaw Leads to Cloud Credential Theft
items.description: Adversaries could plant a malicious repository that can execute arbitrary code and steal cloud credentials by exploiting the vulnerability, which showcases growing MCP risk.

items.title: Why Post-Quantum Cryptography Starts With Credentials
items.description: Today’s encrypted data, such as credentials, may no longer remain confidential in the future because the public-key cryptography protecting it will soon be broken by quantum computers. Although no machine today can break elliptic curve cryptography or RSA, quantum hardware is advancing rapidly and will inevitably change how organizations protect their data. Ciphertext and credentials captured by

items.title: Gamaredon Expands Ukraine Attacks with New Malware and Cloud Service Abuse
items.description: A Russian advanced persistent threat (APT) group has continued to evolve and expand its malware arsenal as part of its ongoing cyber onslaught against Ukraine throughout 2025. Slovakian cybersecurity company ESET said it observed 35 distinct spear-phishing campaigns mounted by Gamaredon against new targets, with most of them taking place in the second half of the year. Primary targets of these

items.title: US seizes hundreds of FIFA World Cup illegal streaming domains
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department's Criminal Division has seized nearly 400 web domains used for illegally streaming matches at the FIFA World Cup. [...]

items.title: ‘DirtyClone’ Linux Kernel Vulnerability Leads to Root Access
items.description: A variant of DirtyFrag, the flaw allows unprivileged local users to manipulate the Linux page cache and gain root privileges. The post ‘DirtyClone’ Linux Kernel Vulnerability Leads to Root Access appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: OpenAI and Anthropic Limit New AI Models to Trump-Approved Customers During Cybersecurity Review
items.description: ChatGPT maker OpenAI said Friday it is restricting the release of its new artificial intelligence model at the request of President Donald Trump’s administration. The post OpenAI and Anthropic Limit New AI Models to Trump-Approved Customers During Cybersecurity Review appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: US Offers $10 Million Bounty for Russian State Hackers as Messaging App Attacks Evolve
items.description: UNC5792 and UNC4221 have been targeting US government officials, military leaders, and allied personnel. The post US Offers $10 Million Bounty for Russian State Hackers as Messaging App Attacks Evolve appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft Removes 119 Edge Extensions That Hid Malware in Images and Fonts
items.description: Microsoft has shut down a long-running malicious extension operation on the Edge Add-ons store that hid its payloads inside ordinary image and font files, then woke up days after install to steal credentials and run ad fraud. The company calls it StegoAd, a mash-up of steganography and adware, and ties 119 extensions to a single threat actor it says has been active since at least 2021.

items.title: OpenAI Unveils GPT-5.6 Sol as Its Most Advanced Cybersecurity AI
items.description: The company says Sol matches competing systems like Mythos Preview while using only a third of the output tokens. The post OpenAI Unveils GPT-5.6 Sol as Its Most Advanced Cybersecurity AI appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Public PoC Released for Critical libssh2 CVE-2026-55200 Client-Side SSH Flaw
items.description: A public proof-of-concept is now out for CVE-2026-55200, a critical flaw in libssh2 that lets a malicious or compromised SSH server trigger memory corruption on a connecting client, with possible code execution. No credentials, no user interaction. The bug affects every release up to and including 1.11.1 and carries a CVSS 4.0 score of 9.2. libssh2 is a client-side SSH library, not a server.

items.title: Hijacked npm and Go Packages Use VS Code Tasks to Deploy Python Infostealer
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered two hijacked npm packages and a cluster of Go packages that are designed to deploy a Python-based information stealer on compromised Windows, Linux, and macOS hosts. "This attack avoids the most common npm execution paths through lifecycle scripts, perhaps in an attempt to remain 'compatible' with npm v12's security hardenings," JFrog said in a

items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Data breach exposes up to 14.2 million email logins at six ISPs
items.description: Japanese telecommunications operator KDDI Corporation disclosed a data breach where threat actors gained access to one of its email systems used by five other internet service providers (ISPs) in the country. [...]

items.title: YARA-X 1.18.0 and 1.19.0 Release, (Sun, Jun 28th)
items.description: YARA-X&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s 1.18.0 release brings 3 improvements and 2 bugfixes.

items.title: Clean GitHub repo tricks AI coding agents into running malware
items.description: An agentic coding tool tasked with cloning and setting up a seemingly benign GitHub repository could execute a malicious payload that remains invisible to security scanners, AI agents, and human reviewers. [...]

items.title: Chinese Framework Powers 200,000 Scam Sites
items.description: Threat actors are selling investment scam templates created using the legitimate DCloud Uni-App toolkit. The post Chinese Framework Powers 200,000 Scam Sites appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Third-Party Breaches Teach Education Sector a Costly Lesson in Vendor Risk
items.description: Rising threats from third-party actors are forcing institutions to play defense to protect student data from ransomware and other attacks.

items.title: AI Decline? Confidence in Autonomous Penetration Testing Falls
items.description: Companies are still experimenting with automated AI systems to find security weaknesses, but fewer are relying on the technology.

items.title: Cisco Adds NHI to Security Stack With Astrix, WideField Acquisitions
items.description: Cisco joins a growing list of security platform providers that are betting that securing the agentic workforce means turning identity into the primary control plane.

items.title: New Initiative Tackles Security for End-of-Life Open Source Software
items.description: The Open Source Sustainability Initiative's goal is to help enterprises manage and secure aging open source projects while maintaining regulatory compliance.

items.title: AI Won't Wipe-Out Entry-Level Cybersecurity Jobs
items.description: Instead of eliminating jobs for early-career cyber pros, AI is creating new opportunities for candidates with strong human decision-making skills.

items.title: Amazon Q Flaw Enabled Cloud Credential Theft via Malicious Repositories
items.description: AWS has patched the vulnerability and published its own advisory to inform customers about the potential impact. The post Amazon Q Flaw Enabled Cloud Credential Theft via Malicious Repositories appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Russia accuses Apple of ‘political censorship’ after VK apps removed from App Store
items.description: Apple removed VK's flagship social network VKontakte, often described as Russia's equivalent of Facebook, along with VK Music, VK Messenger, VK Video, Odnoklassniki and Mail.ru services, including its email application.

items.title: Meeting Trump's 2030 Quantum Deadline Will be Expensive, Complex
items.description: Getting accurate visibility into IT and OT systems will be compounded by multivendor environments, misaligned update life cycles, and interoperability gaps.

items.title: Thanks for Crushing the Submissions Inbox. We're Trying to Keep Up
items.description: It might be taking a bit longer than usual to respond to your submissions — here's why.

items.title: Robinhood Cuts Access Approval Time to Support High-Velocity Development
items.description: The fintech company's engineering-first application security team reengineered the process for granting system access, making it easier and more secure for developers working on their projects. Here are the lessons learned from Robinhood's experience.

items.title: What do Ports Hear When Nobody's Listening? An Assessment of Automated Cybercrime [Guest Diary], (Wed, Jun 24th)
items.description: [This is a Guest Diary by Nicole Phillips, an ISC intern as part of the SANS.edu BACS program]

items.title: Linux Process Name Masquerading, (Wed, Jun 24th)
items.description: In a previous diary, I talked about stack strings&&#x23;x26;&#x23;x5b;1&&#x23;x26;&#x23;x5d; with a practical example of them. Since my SEC670 class, I&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xe2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;x80;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;x99;m even more interested&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xc2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xa0;in malware obfuscation techniques. I had&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xc2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xa0;a look at process names. When you list running processes on a computer, can you trust what you see&&#x23;x26;&#x23;x3f; If you&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;re facing a rootkit, malicious processes can be simply hidden (the API calls or commands to list processed have been tampered). But a malicious process&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xc2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xa0;can also mimic a non-suspicious name by

items.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
items.description: Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. The duo were key members of a prolific cybercrime group known as Scattered Spider, and their guilty pleas came on the first day of what was expected to be a six-week trial.

items.title: CVE-2024-40766: The Patch Fixed the Bug. Nobody Fixed the Configuration., (Tue, Jun 23rd)
items.description: The vulnerability

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, June 23rd, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9982, (Tue, Jun 23rd)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Webshells Remain Popular, (Mon, Jun 22nd)
items.description: Webshells have been popular for a long time. We already covered this topic across multiple diaries[1][2]. I spent some time to track them[3] and slighly paid less attention to them but today I found another one. It seems to be a new player (pushed on Github two months ago).

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Monday, June 22nd, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9980, (Mon, Jun 22nd)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: ‘Popa’ Botnet Linked to Publicly-Traded Israeli Firm
items.description: For the past four years, a sprawling Android-based botnet called Popa has forced millions of consumer TV boxes to relay Internet traffic linked to advertising fraud, account takeovers, and mass data-scraping efforts. This week, researchers from multiple security firms concluded that the Popa botnet is linked to NetNut, a "residential proxy" provider operated by the publicly-traded Israeli firm Alarum Technologies Ltd [NASDAQ: ALAR].

items.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
items.description: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.

items.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
items.description: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesday cycle. Nearly three dozen of those bugs earned Microsoft's most dire "critical" rating, and exploit code for at least three of the weaknesses is now publicly available.

items.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
items.description: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

items.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
items.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.

items.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
items.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
